306 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

‘Aaisha ‘ said, “I was the first among people to ask the Messenger of Allah #% about this verse:

CT Nl a Egcat onl % EAMG a>

“On the Day when the earth will be changed to another earth and so

will be the heavens, and they (all creatures) will appear before Allah,

the One, the Irresistible.’ She 4 said, ‘I said, ‘At that juncture, where are the people, O Messenger of Allah?’ He #¥ said, ‘On the Siraat.’ (Ahmad) Muslim (in his sahih), At-Tirmidhee, and Ibn Maajah related the same hadith through Daawood Ibn Abee Hind. At-Tirmidhee said, “Hasan sahih.”

In another narration, ‘Aaisha % asked about the Verse, saying, “O Messenger of Allah, where are the people at that time?” He 2% said, “They are on the Matn of the Hellfire (ie. on the bridge that is over it).” (Ahmad)

Thaubaan 4s related that a rabbi from the Jews asked the Messenger of Allah 3 about the above-mentioned Verse, saying, “Where will we be when the earth will be changed to another earth and so will the heavens?” The Messenger of Allah #% said, “In darkness, before the bridge (ie. the bridge that is over the Hellfire). (Muslim)

Abu Ayyoob Al-Ansaaree 4 related that a rabbi from the Jews went to the Prophet i% and asked, “Consider the fact that Allah says in His Book: “On the Day when the earth will be changed to another earth and so will be the heavens’, Then where are the created beings at that time?” He #8 said, “The guests of Allah...” (Related by Ibn Jareer) Ibn Abee Haatim related the same.

The above-mentioned change might occur after the gathering of the creation, so that there is a second changing that is different in description from the first. And Allah 4% knows best. ‘Abdul-Kareem (or Abu ‘Abdul-Kareem) said, “I stayed with a man from Khurasaan, and he related to me that he heard ‘Alee ibn Abee Taalib .& say:

At ne AR Kaen ts

ROSES ONT Te ENT OG

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 307

“On the Day when the earth will be changed to another earth and so will be the heavens’

The Lengthy Period Of The Day Of Resurrection

Allah % said: “And they ask you to hasten on the torment! And Allah fails not

His Promise. And verily, a day with your Lord is as a thousand years of what you reckon.”

edo Bh AA eS o ab wh ie faek OL OF GG oh GG MeN GS 403 25 0 4S ES Ao Le he Aho i

“A questioner asked concerning a torment about to befall upon the disbelievers, which none can avert, from Allah, the Lord of the ways of ascent. The angels and Ruh (Jibrael (Gabriel)) ascend to Him ina Day the measure whereof is fifty thousand years, so be patient (O Muhammad 2%), — with a good patience. Verily! They see it (the torment) afar off, but We see it (quite) near.’ When we discussed the Tafseer of this Verse, we mentioned the difference of opinion regarding it among our predecessors and among those in later times as well. As regards to, ‘in a Day the measure whereof is fifty thousand years, Laith Ibn Abee Sulaim

308 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

2 bee ee yi ta gn cal Ml Oak aor Ae 2 oF

“In one Day, the space whereof is a thousand years of your

reckoning (i.e. reckoning of our present world’s time). Ibn ‘Abbaas said, “Meaning, the affair descending from the heavens to the earth and it (the affair) rising from the earth to the heavens, because between the heavens and the earth is the traveling distance of 500 years.” Ibn Abee Haatim and Mujaahid related this narration, and the opinion expressed therein is held by Al-Farraa and Abu ‘Abdullah Al-Hulaimee, according to what is related by AlL-Haafiz Abu Bakr Al-Baihagee in Kitaab Al-Ba'ath Wan-Nushoor. AlHulaimee said, “The angel is able to traverse that distance in part of a day. Even though it is a distance that can be traversed, no one has been able to journey it except in a period of 50,000 years. And in no way is that number (50,000 years) considered to be the duration of the Day of Resurrection. Al-Hulaimee chose this opinion based on the Verse, “From Allah, the Lord of the ways of ascent’. Meaning, Exaltedness and Greatness, similar to Allah’s saying:

“(He is Allah) Oumer of High Ranks and Degrees, the Owner of the Throne.”

According to this first opinion, the meaning of that distance has to do with place (and not passage of time),

According to the second opinion, the meaning of “50,000 years’ is the duration of the world. Abu Muhammad ‘Abdur-Rahmaan ibn. Abee Haatim related the following in his Tafseer. Regarding this Verse, “The measure whereof is fifty thousand years’, Mujaahid said, “The life of this world is 50,000 years. Allah referred to that duration as a day. He &% said, “The angels and Ruh (Jibrael (Gabriel) ascend to Him in a Day.” A day is the World.”

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 309

Regarding ‘the measure whereof is fifty thousand years, ‘AbdurRazzaaq related that ‘Tkrimah said, “From its beginning to its end, the world exists for fifty thousand years. No one among you knows how much of that time has passed by and how much of it remains. Only Allah 4 has knowledge thereof.” Al-Baihagee related the same from Muhammad ibn Thaur. This opinion, however, is very Ghareeb, for it is not found in many of the famous books. And Allah # knows best.

According to the third opinion, the intended meaning is the period between the world and the Day of Resurrection. Ibn Abee Haatim related this from Muhammad ibn Ka’ab Al-Qurazee, and it too is very Ghareeb.

And according to the fourth opinion in this issue, the intended meaning is the length of the Day of Resurrection. Regarding ‘the measure whereof is fifty thousand years,’ ibn Abee Haatim related that Ibn ‘Abbaas + said, “The Day of Resurrection.” The chain of this narration is authentic. In the narration of Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa, Zaid Ar-Raashid said, “The people will stand on the Day of Resurrection for 1000 years and judgment will be issued between them for a period of 10,000 years.

‘Alee Ibn Abee Talhah related the following from Ibn ‘Abbaas 4, “For the disbelievers, Allah made the Day of Resurrection to be the measure of 50,000 years.” In his Tafseer, Al-Kalbee related that Ibn ‘Abbaas said, “Were anyone other than Allah to be given the responsibility of taking (Allah’s) slaves to account, he would not finish the task in 50,000 years (ie, it would take much, much longer).””

Al-Baihagee related that Al-Hasan said, “What do you think about a day in which they stand on their feet for 50 000 years, eating not even a morsel therein, and drinking not even a gulp, until their throats Tagatta’at (literal meaning, are cut off) from the severity of their thirst and their insides are burning with hunger. Then they are taken to the Hellfire and are made to drink from ‘Ain Aaniyah (containers that are extremely hot; the inhabitants of the Hellfire will be made to drink from it), it will reach the pinnacle of hotness and its ripeness will be extreme. This has been related in a number of Alhmadeeth - and Allah knows best.

310 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

In Spite Of The Length And The Severity Of The Day Of Resurrection, It Will Be Lighter For The Believer Than One Of The Compulsory Prayers

Abu Sa’eed said, “It was said to the Messenger of Allah 2%, ‘the day the measurement of which is 50 000 years ~ that is an extremely long day.’ The Messenger of Allah #% said, ‘By the One Who has my soul in His Hand, it is made light for the believer, to the extent that it is more light for him than a compulsory prayer he performs in the world.’” (Ahmad) Ibn Jareer related this in his Tafseer, but two of the narrators of his chain are weak,

Al-Baihagee related it with a different phrasing. Abu Al-Mash related that Abu Saeed Al-Khudree 4s went to the Messenger of Allah 2% and said, “Inform me of he who is strong enough to stand on the Day of Resurrection, about which Allah % said:

“The Day when (all) mankind will stand before the Lord of the Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exist)?” (Quran 83:6)” He 3 said, “Tt is made light for the believer to the extent that it is like a compulsory prayer for him.” ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Amr 2 said, “On the Day of Resurrection, the believers will have chairs of light upon which they will sit, and they will be shaded by clouds above them. For them the Day of Resurrection will be like an hour from the day or like one of its two limits (Le. its beginning or end).” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa in Al-Ahwaal}

Some Of The Punishments That Have Been Prepared For Those Who Do Not Pay The Zakaat That Is Due Upon Them

Abu Hurairah 4 related that the Messenger of Allah 4% said, “There is no possessor of a Kanz (literally, treasure; here it means any wealth for which Zakaat is not paid) who does not pay its right (i.e. Zakaat) except that plates are heated in the Fire of Hell, and then his forehead, his sides, and his back are ironed by them, until Allah rules between His slaves, in a day whose measurement is 50,000 years from what you count. Then he will see his destination —

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations Si

either to Paradise or to the Hellfire.” (Ahmad)

He then mentioned the rest of the hadith, which is about one who refuses to pay Zakaat on sheep and camels. “He will be thrown on his face for them (i.e. for his sheep and camels) on an even, smooth land. He will be trampled over by their Akhfaa (feet of camels) and their Azlaaf (feet of sheep; Azlaaf are also the feet of cows and deer). And they will butt him with their horns. Every time the last of them passes by him, the first one is brought back to do the same, until (Allah) judges between His slaves, on a day whose measurement is 50,000 years from what you count. Then he (that person) will see his destination — either to Paradise or to the Hellfire.” (Ahmad) Abu Daawood and Muslim related the same, each with his respective chain.
content_id:
306 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar

'Aaisha radhiyallahu 'anha berkata, "Aku adalah orang pertama di antara manusia yang bertanya kepada Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam tentang ayat ini:

CT Nl a Egcat onl % EAMG a>

"Pada hari ketika bumi diganti dengan bumi yang lain dan demikian pula langit,

dan mereka (semua makhluk) dihadapkan kepada Allah Yang Maha Esa, Maha Perkasa." Beliau radhiyallahu 'anha berkata, 'Aku berkata, "Pada saat itu, di mana manusia berada, wahai Rasulullah?" Beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Di atas Siraat."' (Ahmad) Muslim (dalam sahihnya), At-Tirmidhee, dan Ibn Maajah meriwayatkan hadith yang sama melalui Daawood Ibn Abee Hind. At-Tirmidhee berkata, "Hasan sahih."

Dalam riwayat lain, 'Aaisha radhiyallahu 'anha bertanya tentang ayat tersebut, "Wahai Rasulullah, di mana manusia pada saat itu?" Beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Mereka berada di Matn Jahannam (yakni di atas jembatan yang melintasinya)." (Ahmad)

Thaubaan radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa seorang rabbi dari kalangan Yahudi bertanya kepada Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam tentang ayat yang disebutkan di atas, "Di manakah kami berada ketika bumi diganti dengan bumi yang lain dan demikian pula langit?" Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Dalam kegelapan, sebelum jembatan (yakni jembatan yang melintasi Jahannam)." (Muslim)

Abu Ayyoob Al-Ansaaree radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa seorang rabbi dari kalangan Yahudi mendatangi Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam dan bertanya, "Renungkanlah bahwa Allah berfirman dalam Kitab-Nya: 'Pada hari ketika bumi diganti dengan bumi yang lain dan demikian pula langit', Lalu di manakah para makhluk pada saat itu?" Beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Mereka adalah tamu-tamu Allah..." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Jareer) Ibn Abee Haatim meriwayatkan hal yang sama.

Perubahan yang disebutkan di atas mungkin terjadi setelah dikumpulkannya seluruh makhluk, sehingga terdapat perubahan kedua yang berbeda deskripsinya dari yang pertama. Dan Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala lebih mengetahui. 'Abdul-Kareem (atau Abu 'Abdul-Kareem) berkata, "Aku tinggal bersama seorang laki-laki dari Khurasan, dan ia meriwayatkan kepadaku bahwa ia mendengar 'Alee ibn Abee Taalib radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata:

At ne AR Kaen ts

ROSES ONT Te ENT OG

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar 307

"Pada hari ketika bumi diganti dengan bumi yang lain dan demikian pula langit'"

Panjangnya Hari Kiamat

Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala berfirman: "Dan mereka meminta kepadamu agar azab itu disegerakan! Padahal Allah tidak akan menyalahi janji-Nya. Dan sesungguhnya satu hari di sisi Tuhanmu adalah seperti seribu tahun menurut perhitunganmu."

edo Bh AA eS o ab wh ie faek OL OF GG oh GG MeN GS 403 25 0 4S ES Ao Le he Aho i

"Seorang peminta meminta datangnya azab yang akan menimpa orang-orang kafir, yang tidak seorang pun dapat menolaknya, dari Allah, Tuhan segala tempat naik. Para malaikat dan Ruh (Jibril 'alaihissalam) naik kepada-Nya dalam sehari yang kadarnya lima puluh ribu tahun. Maka bersabarlah engkau (wahai Muhammad shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) dengan kesabaran yang baik. Sesungguhnya mereka memandang azab itu jauh, sedang Kami memandangnya dekat." Ketika kami membahas tafsir ayat ini, kami menyebutkan perbedaan pendapat mengenainya di antara para pendahulu kita dan mereka yang datang setelahnya. Adapun mengenai "yang kadarnya lima puluh ribu tahun", Laith Ibn Abee Sulaim

308 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar

2 bee ee yi ta gn cal Ml Oak aor Ae 2 oF

"Dalam satu hari yang kadarnya seribu tahun menurut

perhitunganmu (yakni perhitungan waktu dunia kita ini). Ibn 'Abbaas berkata, "Maksudnya, urusan yang turun dari langit ke bumi dan (urusan itu) naik dari bumi ke langit, karena antara langit dan bumi adalah jarak perjalanan 500 tahun." Ibn Abee Haatim dan Mujaahid meriwayatkan narasi ini, dan pendapat yang dikemukakan di dalamnya dipegang oleh Al-Farraa dan Abu 'Abdullah Al-Hulaimee, berdasarkan apa yang diriwayatkan oleh Al-Haafiz Abu Bakr Al-Baihagee dalam Kitaab Al-Ba'ath Wan-Nushoor. Al-Hulaimee berkata, "Malaikat mampu menempuh jarak tersebut dalam sebagian hari. Meskipun itu adalah jarak yang dapat ditempuh, tidak ada seorang pun yang mampu menempuhnya kecuali dalam kurun waktu 50.000 tahun. Dan angka tersebut (50.000 tahun) sama sekali bukan dianggap sebagai lamanya Hari Kiamat. Al-Hulaimee memilih pendapat ini berdasarkan ayat, "dari Allah, Tuhan segala tempat naik". Artinya, Keagungan dan Kebesaran, serupa dengan firman Allah:

"(Dia adalah Allah) Pemilik derajat-derajat yang tinggi, Penguasa 'Arsy."

Menurut pendapat pertama ini, makna dari jarak tersebut berkaitan dengan tempat (dan bukan berlalunya waktu).

Menurut pendapat kedua, makna "50.000 tahun" adalah lamanya dunia. Abu Muhammad 'Abdur-Rahmaan ibn Abee Haatim meriwayatkan hal berikut dalam tafsirnya. Mengenai ayat ini, "yang kadarnya lima puluh ribu tahun", Mujaahid berkata, "Kehidupan dunia ini adalah 50.000 tahun. Allah menyebut durasi tersebut sebagai satu hari. Beliau subhanahu wa ta'ala berfirman, 'Para malaikat dan Ruh (Jibril 'alaihissalam) naik kepada-Nya dalam satu hari.' Satu hari adalah dunia."

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar 309

Mengenai "yang kadarnya lima puluh ribu tahun", 'AbdurRazzaaq meriwayatkan bahwa 'Ikrimah berkata, "Dari awal hingga akhirnya, dunia ada selama lima puluh ribu tahun. Tidak ada seorang pun di antara kalian yang mengetahui berapa banyak waktu itu yang telah berlalu dan berapa yang tersisa. Hanya Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala yang memiliki pengetahuan tentang hal itu." Al-Baihagee meriwayatkan hal yang sama dari Muhammad ibn Thaur. Namun pendapat ini sangat Ghareeb, karena tidak ditemukan dalam banyak kitab-kitab terkenal. Dan Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala lebih mengetahui.

Menurut pendapat ketiga, makna yang dimaksud adalah periode antara dunia dan Hari Kiamat. Ibn Abee Haatim meriwayatkan ini dari Muhammad ibn Ka'ab Al-Qurazee, dan ini pun sangat Ghareeb.

Dan menurut pendapat keempat dalam masalah ini, makna yang dimaksud adalah lamanya Hari Kiamat. Mengenai "yang kadarnya lima puluh ribu tahun", Ibn Abee Haatim meriwayatkan bahwa Ibn 'Abbaas radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata, "Hari Kiamat." Sanad riwayat ini adalah sahih. Dalam riwayat Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa, Zaid Ar-Raashid berkata, "Manusia akan berdiri pada Hari Kiamat selama 1.000 tahun dan pengadilan akan berlangsung di antara mereka selama 10.000 tahun."

'Alee Ibn Abee Talhah meriwayatkan hal berikut dari Ibn 'Abbaas radhiyallahu 'anhu, "Bagi orang-orang kafir, Allah menjadikan Hari Kiamat berukuran 50.000 tahun." Dalam tafsirnya, Al-Kalbee meriwayatkan bahwa Ibn 'Abbaas berkata, "Seandainya selain Allah diberi tanggung jawab untuk menghisab hamba-hamba (Allah), ia tidak akan selesai dalam 50.000 tahun (yakni akan membutuhkan waktu yang jauh, jauh lebih lama)."

Al-Baihagee meriwayatkan bahwa Al-Hasan berkata, "Apa yang kau pikirkan tentang sebuah hari di mana mereka berdiri di atas kaki mereka selama 50.000 tahun, tidak memakan satu suap pun di dalamnya, dan tidak meminum satu teguk pun, hingga tenggorokan mereka Tagatta'at (makna harfiah, terputus) karena beratnya dahaga dan perut mereka terbakar karena lapar. Kemudian mereka dibawa ke Jahannam dan diberi minum dari 'Ain Aaniyah (wadah-wadah yang sangat panas; penghuni Jahannam akan diberi minum darinya), ia akan mencapai puncak panasnya dan kematangannya sangat ekstrem. Hal ini telah diriwayatkan dalam sejumlah hadith — dan Allah lebih mengetahui.

310 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar

Meskipun Panjang dan Beratnya Hari Kiamat, Ia Akan Terasa Lebih Ringan bagi Orang Mukmin daripada Salah Satu Salah Wajib

Abu Sa'eed berkata, "Dikatakan kepada Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, 'Hari yang ukurannya 50.000 tahun — itu adalah hari yang sangat panjang.' Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, 'Demi Dzat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, hari itu diringankan bagi orang mukmin, hingga ia lebih ringan baginya daripada salah wajib yang ia kerjakan di dunia.'" (Ahmad) Ibn Jareer meriwayatkan ini dalam tafsirnya, namun dua perawi dalam sanadnya adalah lemah.

Al-Baihagee meriwayatkannya dengan redaksi yang berbeda. Abu Al-Mash meriwayatkan bahwa Abu Saeed Al-Khudree radhiyallahu 'anhu mendatangi Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam dan berkata, "Beritahukan kepadaku tentang orang yang mampu berdiri pada Hari Kiamat, yang mana Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala berfirman tentangnya:

"Hari ketika (seluruh) manusia berdiri di hadapan Tuhan semesta alam (manusia, jin, dan semua yang ada)?" (Quran 83:6)" Beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Hari itu diringankan bagi orang mukmin hingga seperti salah wajib baginya." 'Abdullah Ibn 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata, "Pada Hari Kiamat, orang-orang mukmin akan memiliki kursi-kursi dari cahaya yang akan mereka duduki, dan mereka akan ternaungi oleh awan di atas mereka. Bagi mereka, Hari Kiamat akan seperti satu jam dari hari atau seperti salah satu dari dua batasnya (yakni awal atau akhirnya)." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa dalam Al-Ahwaal)

Beberapa Hukuman Yang Telah Disiapkan Bagi Mereka Yang Tidak Membayar Zakah Yang Wajib Atas Mereka

Abu Hurairah radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Tidak ada seorang pun pemilik Kanz (secara harfiah berarti harta simpanan; di sini bermakna harta apa pun yang tidak dibayarkan zakatnya) yang tidak menunaikan haknya (yaitu zakah) kecuali lempeng-lempeng akan dipanaskan dalam api Neraka, lalu dahi, lambung, dan punggungnya disetrika dengan lempeng-lempeng tersebut, hingga Allah menghakimi di antara hamba-hamba-Nya, pada suatu hari yang ukurannya adalah 50.000 tahun dari apa yang kalian hitung. Kemudian ia akan melihat tempat tujuannya —

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar

apakah ke Surga atau ke Neraka." (Ahmad)

Beliau kemudian menyebutkan sisa hadith tersebut, yang berkenaan dengan orang yang enggan membayar zakah atas kambing dan unta. "Ia akan dilemparkan dengan wajahnya menghadap ke bawah untuk mereka (yaitu untuk kambing dan untanya) di atas tanah yang rata dan datar. Ia akan diinjak-injak oleh Akhfaa mereka (kaki-kaki unta) dan Azlaaf mereka (kaki-kaki kambing; Azlaaf juga merupakan kaki sapi dan rusa). Dan mereka akan menanduknya dengan tanduk-tanduk mereka. Setiap kali yang terakhir dari mereka melewatinya, yang pertama didatangkan kembali untuk melakukan hal yang sama, hingga (Allah) menghakimi di antara hamba-hamba-Nya, pada suatu hari yang ukurannya adalah 50.000 tahun dari apa yang kalian hitung. Kemudian ia (orang tersebut) akan melihat tempat tujuannya — apakah ke Surga atau ke Neraka." (Ahmad) Abu Dawud dan Muslim meriwayatkan hal yang sama, masing-masing dengan sanadnya tersendiri.
content_ar: 306 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations ‘Aaisha ‘ said, “I was the أولاً among people to ask الرسول ﷺ of الله #% about this الآية: CT Nl a Egcat onl % EAMG a> “On the Day عندما the earth will be changed to another earth and لذا will be the heavens, and they (all creatures) will appear قبل الله, the One, the Irresistible.’ She 4 said, ‘I said, ‘At that juncture, where are the people, O Messenger of الله?’ He #¥ said, ‘On the Siraat.’ (أحمد) مسلم (in his صحيح), At-Tirmidhee, and Ibn Maajah related the same الحديث through Daawood Ibn Abee Hind. At-Tirmidhee said, “حسن صحيح.” In another narration, ‘Aaisha % asked about the الآية, saying, “O Messenger of الله, where are the people at that time?” He 2% said, “They are on the المتن of the النار (ie. on the bridge that is over it).” (أحمد) Thaubaan 4s related that a rabbi from the Jews asked الرسول ﷺ of الله 3 about the above-mentioned الآية, saying, “Where will we be عندما the earth will be changed to another earth and لذا will the heavens?” الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, “In darkness, قبل the bridge (ie. the bridge that is over the النار). (مسلم) Abu Ayyoob Al-Ansaaree 4 related that a rabbi from the Jews went to النبي ﷺ i% and asked, “Consider the fact that الله says in His الكتاب: “On the Day عندما the earth will be changed to another earth and لذا will be the heavens’, Then where are the created beings at that time?” He #8 said, “The guests of الله...” (Related by Ibn Jareer) Ibn Abee Haatim related the same. The above-mentioned change might occur بعد the gathering of the creation, لذا that there is a ثانياً changing that is different in description from the أولاً. And الله 4% knows best. ‘Abdul-Kareem (or Abu ‘Abdul-Kareem) said, “I stayed with a man from Khurasaan, and he related to me that he heard ‘Alee ibn Abee Taalib .& say: At ne AR Kaen ts ROSES ONT Te ENT OG الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 307 “On the Day عندما the earth will be changed to another earth and لذا will be the heavens’ The Lengthy Period Of The Day Of البعث الله % said: “And they ask you to hasten on the torment! And الله fails not His Promise. And verily, a day with your Lord is as a thousand years of what you reckon.” edo Bh AA eS o ab wh ie faek OL OF GG oh GG MeN GS 403 25 0 4S ES Ao Le he Aho i “A questioner asked فيما يتعلق بـ a torment about to befall upon the disbelievers, which none can avert, from الله, the Lord of the ways of ascent. The الملائكة and Ruh (Jibrael (جبريل ﷺ)) ascend to Him ina Day the measure whereof is fifty thousand years, لذا be patient (O محمد ﷺ 2%), — with a الخير الصبر. Verily! They see it (the torment) afar off, but We see it (quite) near.’ عندما we discussed the Tafseer of this الآية, we mentioned the difference of opinion فيما يخص it among our predecessors and among those in later times as well. As regards to, ‘in a Day the measure whereof is fifty thousand years, Laith Ibn Abee Sulaim 308 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 2 bee ee yi ta gn cal Ml Oak aor Ae 2 oF “In يوماً ما, the space whereof is a thousand years of your reckoning (i.e. reckoning of our present الدنيا’s time). Ibn ‘Abbaas said, “Meaning, the affair descending from the heavens to the earth and it (the affair) rising from the earth to the heavens, لأن between the heavens and the earth is the traveling distance of 500 years.” Ibn Abee Haatim and Mujaahid related this narration, and the opinion expressed therein is held by Al-Farraa and Abu ‘Abdullah Al-Hulaimee, وفقاً لـ what is related by AlL-Haafiz أبو بكر Al-Baihagee in Kitaab Al-Ba'ath Wan-Nushoor. AlHulaimee said, “The angel is able to traverse that distance in part of a day. حتى وإن it is a distance that can be traversed, no one has been able to journey it except in a period of 50,000 years. And in no الطريق is that number (50,000 years) considered to be the duration of the Day of البعث. Al-Hulaimee chose this opinion based on the الآية, “From الله, the Lord of the ways of ascent’. Meaning, Exaltedness and Greatness, similar to الله’s saying: “(He is الله) Oumer of High Ranks and Degrees, the Owner of the Throne.” وفقاً لـ this أولاً opinion, the meaning of that distance has to do with place (and not passage of time), وفقاً لـ the ثانياً opinion, the meaning of “50,000 years’ is the duration of the الدنيا. Abu محمد ﷺ ‘Abdur-Rahmaan ibn. Abee Haatim related the following in his Tafseer. فيما يخص this الآية, “The measure whereof is fifty thousand years’, Mujaahid said, “The الحياة of this الدنيا is 50,000 years. الله referred to that duration as a day. He &% said, “The الملائكة and Ruh (Jibrael (جبريل ﷺ) ascend to Him in a Day.” A day is the الدنيا.” الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 309 فيما يخص ‘the measure whereof is fifty thousand years, ‘AbdurRazzaaq related that ‘Tkrimah said, “From its beginning to its end, the الدنيا exists for fifty thousand years. No one among you knows how much of that time has passed by and how much of it remains. Only الله 4 has العلم thereof.” Al-Baihagee related the same from محمد ﷺ ibn Thaur. This opinion, غير أن, is very Ghareeb, for it is not found in many of the famous الكتب. And الله # knows best. وفقاً لـ the ثالثاً opinion, the intended meaning is the period between the الدنيا and the Day of البعث. Ibn Abee Haatim related this from محمد ﷺ ibn Ka’ab Al-Qurazee, and it أيضاً is very Ghareeb. And وفقاً لـ the رابعاً opinion in this issue, the intended meaning is the length of the Day of البعث. فيما يخص ‘the measure whereof is fifty thousand years,’ ibn Abee Haatim related that Ibn ‘Abbaas + said, “The Day of البعث.” The chain of this narration is authentic. In the narration of Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa, Zaid Ar-Raashid said, “The people will stand on the Day of البعث for 1000 years and judgment will be issued between them for a period of 10,000 years. ‘Alee Ibn Abee Talhah related the following from Ibn ‘Abbaas 4, “For the disbelievers, الله made the Day of البعث to be the measure of 50,000 years.” In his Tafseer, Al-Kalbee related that Ibn ‘Abbaas said, “Were anyone other than الله to be given the المسؤولية of taking (الله’s) slaves to account, he would not finish the task in 50,000 years (ie, it would take much, much longer).”” Al-Baihagee related that Al-حسن said, “What do you think about a day in which they stand on their feet for 50 000 years, eating not even a morsel therein, and drinking not even a gulp, حتى their throats Tagatta’at (literal meaning, are cut off) from the severity of their thirst and their insides are burning with hunger. Then they are taken to the النار and are made to drink from ‘Ain Aaniyah (containers that are extremely hot; the inhabitants of the النار will be made to drink from it), it will reach the pinnacle of hotness and its ripeness will be extreme. This has been related in a number of Alhmadeeth - and الله knows best. 310 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations In Spite Of The Length And The Severity Of The Day Of البعث, It Will Be Lighter For The المؤمن Than One Of The Compulsory الصلوات Abu Sa’eed said, “It was said to الرسول ﷺ of الله 2%, ‘the day the measurement of which is 50 000 years ~ that is an extremely long day.’ الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, ‘By the One Who has my النفس in His Hand, it is made light for the المؤمن, to the extent that it is more light for him than a compulsory الصلاة he performs in the الدنيا.’” (أحمد) Ibn Jareer related this in his Tafseer, but two of the narrators of his chain are weak, Al-Baihagee related it with a different phrasing. Abu Al-Mash related that Abu Saeed Al-Khudree 4s went to الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% and said, “Inform me of he who is strong enough to stand on the Day of البعث, about which الله % said: “The Day عندما (all) mankind will stand قبل the Lord of the Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exist)?” (القرآن الكريم 83:6)” He 3 said, “Tt is made light for the المؤمن to the extent that it is like a compulsory الصلاة for him.” ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Amr 2 said, “On the Day of البعث, the المؤمنون will have chairs of light upon which they will sit, and they will be shaded by clouds above them. For them the Day of البعث will be like an hour from the day or like one of its two limits (Le. its beginning or end).” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa in Al-Ahwaal} Some Of The Punishments That Have Been Prepared For Those Who Do Not Pay The Zakaat That Is Due Upon Them أبو هريرة 4 related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 4% said, “There is no possessor of a Kanz (literally, treasure; here it means any الثروة for which Zakaat is not paid) who does not pay its الحق (i.e. Zakaat) except that plates are heated in the Fire of النار, and then his forehead, his sides, and his back are ironed by them, حتى الله rules between His slaves, in a day whose measurement is 50,000 years from what you count. Then he will see his destination — الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations Si either to الجنة or to the النار.” (أحمد) He then mentioned the rest of the الحديث, which is about one who refuses to pay Zakaat on sheep and camels. “He will be thrown on his face for them (i.e. for his sheep and camels) on an even, smooth land. He will be trampled over by their Akhfaa (feet of camels) and their Azlaaf (feet of sheep; Azlaaf are أيضاً the feet of cows and deer). And they will butt him with their horns. Every time the last of them passes by him, the أولاً one is brought back to do the same, حتى (الله) judges between His slaves, on a day whose measurement is 50,000 years from what you count. Then he (that person) will see his destination — either to الجنة or to the النار.” (أحمد) Abu Daawood and مسلم related the same, each with his respective chain.
